meaning: n.(=concertmeister) (交响乐队的)首席小提琴手
examples: Concertmaster: The first violinist and assistant conductor in a symphony orchestra.
For each piece the orchestra will perform, a single member is elected as concertmaster.
Was founded in 1996 by the outstanding virtuoso violinist Jan Stanienda who is the orchestra's concertmaster, as well as its conductor.
Associate concertmaster Dawn Harms has to juggle her position here with one in the San Francisco Opera Orchestra.
In the early years, the rotating position of concertmaster was democratically elected for each concert, leading to intense lobbying.
